steps.title: Match problem to response
steps.content: Text 1 presents the problem: countries define urban settlements differently, so researchers have trouble comparing urbanization across countries. Text 2 responds by describing a new "degree of urbanization" measure that uses global criteria for cities, towns, and rural areas. That measure directly addresses the inconsistency described in Text 1.

steps.title: Rule out unrelated responses
steps.content:
• Choice A - By suggesting that researchers focus on topics besides urbanization: Text 2 keeps the focus on urbanization and improves the way it can be studied.
  • Choice B - By noting that a possible solution to the problem is available: the global measure supplies common criteria, which is exactly the kind of solution the comparison problem needs.
  • Choice C - By recommending that a specific institution should further investigate the problem: Text 2 says organizations already developed a measure, not that one institution should investigate.
  • Choice D - By supplying additional ways in which urbanization research is difficult: Text 2 reduces the difficulty rather than adding a new one.
The answer is B.
